items.situation: two classmates discussing a group project deadline
items.speaker: Why are we redoing the whole slide deck again?

options.A: Beats me — nobody told me either.
options.B: The library closes at nine tonight.
options.D: Sometime after the meeting, I think.

items.explanation: 正确答案是A。说话人问“为什么”重做幻灯片，A用习语“Beats me”（我也不知道）自然回应并表示同样困惑，属 idiomatic。B是 off_topic，只谈图书馆关门时间，与问题无关。C是 semantic_association_trap，由“slide deck”联想到幻灯片好看，却没回答为什么。D是 wrong_question_type，回答的是“什么时候”而非“为什么”。

items.situation: student asking about a reading list for a course
items.speaker: I still can't find the reading list anywhere.

options.A: That novel was a great read.
options.B: Actually, it's pinned on the course website.
options.C: The cafeteria stops serving at eight.
options.D: No, I haven't returned it yet.

items.explanation: 正确答案是B。说话人抱怨找不到阅读清单，B用话语标记“Actually”开头，间接告知它就在课程网站上，属 marker_led_indirect。A是 semantic_association_trap，由“reading”联想到小说。C是 off_topic，谈食堂时间，毫无关联。D是 wrong_question_type，回答的是“是否归还”而非“在哪里找”。

items.situation: checking if a service is open
items.speaker: How late's the post office open today?

options.B: I need to mail a package.
options.C: Well, they usually close around five.

items.explanation: 正确答案是C。说话人问邮局开到多晚，C用“Well”开头间接给出大自五点关门，属 marker_led_indirect。A是 wrong_question_type，回答的是“在哪里”而非“开到多晚”。B是 semantic_association_trap，由“post office”联想到寄包裹。D是 off_topic，谈表弟工作，无关。

items.speaker: Didn't the drilling next door keep you up?

options.A: The recycling goes out on Tuesdays.
options.B: They're putting up a new fence.
options.C: It's coming from the third floor.
options.D: Honestly, I slept right through it.

items.explanation: 正确答案是D。说话人问钻孔声是否吵得邻居睡不着，D用“Honestly”开头，间接表示自己一觉到天亮（含义“没被吵到”），属 marker_led_indirect。A是 off_topic，谈回收日程。B是 semantic_association_trap，由“drilling next door”联想到施工建围栏。C是 wrong_question_type，回答的是“声音从哪来”而非“是否吵到你”。

items.situation: student checking with a classmate about lab report requirements
items.speaker: Where am I supposed to submit the lab report?

options.A: Your guess is as good as mine.

items.explanation: 正确答案是A。说话人问实验报告交到哪里，A用习语“Your guess is as good as mine”（我也不清楚）自然回应，属 idiomatic。B是 semantic_association_trap，由“lab report”联想到实验室设备。C是 off_topic，谈训练时间。D是 wrong_question_type，回答的是“什么时候交”而非“交到哪里”。

items.speaker: Aren't you open for another hour or so?

options.B: Actually, we're closing in ten minutes.
options.C: The parking lot is being repaved.

items.explanation: 正确答案是B。说话人问是否还开一个小时，B用“Actually”开头，间接告知十分钟后就关门（含义“开不了那么久”），属 marker_led_indirect。A是 semantic_association_trap，由商店联想到卖什么。C是 off_topic，谈停车场重铺。D是 wrong_question_type，回答的是“开车多久”而非“是否还开着”。

items.situation: student discussing a presentation topic with a partner
items.speaker: What's a good topic for our presentation?

options.A: The projector in that room is broken.
options.B: I finally returned those library books.
options.C: How about renewable energy in cities?

items.explanation: 正确答案是C。说话人问该选什么选题，C用反问“How about...?”提出具体选题并推进对话，属 counter_question。A是 semantic_association_trap，由“presentation”联想到投影仪。B是 off_topic，谈归还图书。D是 wrong_question_type，回答的是“什么时候”而非“选什么题”。

items.situation: asking for directions to a nearby location
items.speaker: I'm trying to find the nearest pharmacy.

options.A: The weather turned cold this morning.
options.B: I take that same medicine too.
options.D: Are you okay with a short walk?

items.explanation: 正确答案是D。说话人说想找最近的药店，D用反问“Are you okay with a short walk?”推进对话、准备指路，属 counter_question。A是 off_topic，谈天气。B是 semantic_association_trap，由“pharmacy”联想到自己也吃同样的药。C是 wrong_question_type，回答的是“几点开门”而非“在哪里”。

items.situation: student asking a classmate about a midterm exam
items.speaker: When's the midterm actually starting on Monday?

options.A: The exam covers the first five chapters.
options.B: Well, the email said nine sharp.
options.C: My roommate adopted a kitten yesterday.
options.D: In the big lecture hall, I think.

items.explanation: 正确答案是B。说话人问期中考周一几点开始，B用“Well”开头，间接引用邮件告知九点整，属 marker_led_indirect。A是 semantic_association_trap，由“midterm”联想到考试范围。C是 off_topic，谈室友领养小猫。D是 wrong_question_type，回答的是“在哪里考”而非“几点考”。

items.situation: someone struggling to open a jar in the kitchen
items.speaker: I can't get this jar to open.

options.A: Here, let me give it a try.
options.C: The bus was late again today.

items.explanation: 正确答案是A。说话人抱怨杆子拧不开，A不去回答字面问题而直接上手帮忙解决困难，属 context_shift。B是 semantic_association_trap，由“jar”联想到果酱好吃。C是 off_topic，谈公交车晚点。D是 wrong_question_type，回答的是“是否买了”而非帮忙开盖。
